---
title: "The Rev. Canon James Duncan"
---

## The Rev. Canon James Duncan

**Dates:** (1839-1895)

### Description

secretary of the National Society

### Biography

Ordained (1863) by the Bishop of Winchester, he served as curate of Alverstoke, Hants 1862-67, and Christ Church, Albany Street, London 1867-70. He served as secretary of the National Society for the Education of the Poor in Accordance with the Principles of the Established Church from 1871 till 1891 when he resigned to become a canon of Canterbury.  [MS Lambeth Palace 4503 ff.63-5, letter from E.W. Benson; Crockfords; brief obituary <i>Leeds Mercury</i> (12 January 1895); canonry <i>Daily News</i> 26 May 1891.]

### Letters

- Letter from Feb 12th 1892
- Letter from March 19th [1887]
- Letter from June 9th 1882
- Letter from Jan 5th 1882
- Letter from Nov 30th [or 3rd?] 1880

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
